title: Tevin Heath
text:
Hello world, I am a Computer Engineering Student at the University of Guelph. My professional interests focuses on the software and hardware design of renewable energy tools and equipment for applied trades workers such as mechanics and electricians. I also have a strong interest in assisting the tradesmen in the transition and designing support tools for the emerging and ever growing field of renewable innovative solutions such as solar electricity, green-fuel technologies, and renewable methods of transportation.
Prior to the attending the University of Guelph, I completed my Honours Bachelor of Science (B.Sc) degree from McMaster University in Mathematics and Statistics.
At the University of Guelph, I mentor and assist in group projects and volunteer in coding, design, and engineering competition, while balancing my intensive coursework and engineering skillet on a daily basis.
Feel free to book an appointment or send me an email, I am always open to chat, mentor, and share towards the many challenges the future has within the field of renewable hardware technologies and software’s.
Download my resume (here)

title: Projects V2
text:
My projects aims at…
Through this lens, my projects can be divided into four intersecting areas: (1) Electrical Engineering (2) model selection, (3) high-dimensional and nonparametric theory, and (4) applications related to these. My work explores and exploits the connections between these areas rather than approaching them separately—my contributions have been developed out of the pressing need to justify methodology as implemented in applications rather than in a vacuum devoid of empirical motivation. My research program seeks to generate statistical guarantees for the procedures that applied researchers use while also developing methodology for complicated, high-dimensional problems. Within this context, much of my work involves what is referred to as regularization—the process of mathematically balancing complex but meaningful scientific models with a preference for simple fundamental structures.

title: Education
text:
Hello world, I’m Tevin - a first-year Bachelor of Engineering Student studying Computer Engineering from the University of Guelph. I am deeply passionate about technology, engineering and control system, and mentoring. I am would like to make the world a better place, while inspiring other to do the same.


Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Engineering (Co-op)

The University of Guelph (Sep. 2023 - PRESENT)

Interests:

Hardware Design & Modelling
Control System Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Computer System Design & Networks





Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Mathematics & Statistics

McMaster University (Sep. 2016 -  Jul 2020)

Interests:

Data Science
Machine Learning
Applied Statistics & Biostatitics
Time Series Modelling
Statistical Programming





Internship Experience


Data and Decision Support Intern, Cambridge Memorial Hospital (June 2022 to Sept 2022)
Business & Data Analyst Intern, Homewood Health Inc. (April 2022 - June 2022)
Senior Consultant Intern, KPMG Canada (October 2021 - May 2022)
Municipal Supervisor Intern, City of London, Ontario (June 2021 - October 2021)



Programming Languages

C/C++
C#
HTML/CSS
Java
KQL/SQL
PHP
Python

title: Courses (Guelph Computer Engineering)
text:
Thank you for visiting my courses pages. Feel free to explore the courses that I have enrolled in throuhgout studies as a Computer Engineering Student at the University of Guelph. This page aims at providing the different courses throughout the University of Guelph Engineering Computer Engineering Program. Note that first-year is general and provides the foundational sciences along with a deep dive into the engineering profession.
For potential employer and recruiters interest:
